close_reason:
End-to-end verified:
- nix eval .#lib.defaultTellerLib --apply 'l: l ? resolveTool' → true
- nix eval .#lib.defaultTellerRegistry --apply 'f: (f "aarch64-darwin") ? buck2' → true
- turnkey's own 'tk build //src/python/... //src/examples/python-hello-deps:python-hello-deps' BUILD SUCCEEDED
- Throwaway downstream flake with bare 'turnkey.toolchains = { enable = true; declarationFiles.default = ...; }' (no tellerLib/tellerRegistry) parses and resolves the turnkey input cleanly.
- Template flake validates likewise.
Full devShells evaluation via 'nix eval' can't be tested due to devenv's CWD limitation, but Buck2/direnv path covers the realistic user flow.
description:
End-to-end verification gate:

- 'nix eval .#lib.defaultTellerLib' resolves to teller's lib.
- 'nix eval .#lib.defaultTellerRegistry --apply "f: f \"aarch64-darwin\""' returns an attrset (registry).
- 'nix develop' / direnv reload produces a working turnkey dev shell.
- 'tk build //...' still BUILD SUCCEEDED.
- A throwaway consumer flake importing inputs.turnkey.flakeModules.turnkey with only 'turnkey.toolchains = { enable = true; declarationFiles.default = ./toolchain.toml; buck2 = {...}; }' (no tellerLib, no tellerRegistry) evaluates and produces a working shell.
- Template-instantiated flake evaluates cleanly.
title: Verify defaults flow end-to-end
